entries.text: Rain is water that falls from clouds. The Sun heats water in seas and lakes; it goes up as vapour and forms clouds. When the clouds get heavy, drops fall as rain. Rain helps plants grow.
Snow is frozen rain. When it is very cold in the cloud, water freezes into tiny ice crystals and falls as snow. Each snowflake has its own shape. Snow is fun to play in but wear warm clothes.

Thunder is the loud sound you hear during a storm. Lightning is a flash of light in the sky. They happen together but we see lightning before we hear thunder because light travels faster than sound. Inside is the safest place in a storm.
question_hints: Why do we see lightning first
What to do in a storm

entries.text: A rainbow appears when sunlight passes through rain or water in the air. The light splits into colours: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet. You often see a rainbow when the Sun comes out after rain.
question_hints: What colours are in a rainbow

entries.text: Wind is moving air. When air gets warm it rises and cooler air moves in. That movement is wind. A gentle wind is nice; a strong wind can make trees sway. Wind can turn windmills and fly kites.
We have four seasons because Earth is slightly tilted. When your part of Earth tilts toward the Sun it is summer; when it tilts away it is winter. Spring and autumn are in between.
question_hints: Why is it hot in summer
